title: San Francisco 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: San Francisco 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to San Francisco, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Alcatraz Island and California Academy of Sciences. Prices from $2796 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering San Francisco, United States — Cool Mediterranean/maritime climate: mild, often foggy summers (highs just 18-19°C due to the marine layer, famously colder than spring/fall) and mild, wetter winters (highs 13-14°C, most of the year's rain falls Nov-Mar). September to November for the city's warmest, clearest weather (highs 20-23°C, after summer fog clears); summer (Jun-Aug) is notoriously cool and foggy despite being "peak season" elsewhere. This itinerary blends Alcatraz Island, California Academy of Sciences, Golden Gate Bridge with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Alcatraz Island, California Academy of Sciences, Golden Gate Bridge
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in San Francisco
itinerary.description: Land at San Francisco International Airport (SFO) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Alcatraz Island — Former federal prison island, reached by ferry, with an acclaimed audio tour. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: San Francisco: California Academy of Sciences & de Young Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to California Academy of Sciences — Aquarium, planetarium, natural history museum and living roof in Golden Gate Park. In the afternoon, continue to de Young Museum — Fine arts museum in Golden Gate Park with a free-to-ascend observation tower. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: San Francisco: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art (SFMOMA) & Exploratorium
itinerary.description: Morning visit to San Francisco Museum of Modern Art (SFMOMA) — Major modern and contemporary art collection. In the afternoon, continue to Exploratorium — Hands-on science museum on the Embarcadero waterfront. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art (SFMOMA)

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Napa Valley wine country
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Napa Valley wine country, a popular side trip from San Francisco. Return to San Francisco in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Napa Valley wine country

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Sutro Baths ruins — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: San Francisco: San Francisco Zoo & Cable car all-day Muni Passport
itinerary.description: Morning visit to San Francisco Zoo — Zoo near Ocean Beach with over 250 species. In the afternoon, continue to Cable car all-day Muni Passport — Unlimited rides on the historic cable car lines. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Muir Woods National Monument
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Muir Woods National Monument, a popular side trip from San Francisco. Return to San Francisco in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Muir Woods National Monument

itinerary.title: San Francisco: Golden Gate Bridge & Golden Gate Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Golden Gate Bridge — Iconic Art Deco suspension bridge, viewable from multiple free vista points. In the afternoon, continue to Golden Gate Park — Sprawling urban park larger than New York's Central Park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Land's End coastal trail — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Sausalito
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Sausalito, a popular side trip from San Francisco. Return to San Francisco in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: San Francisco: Painted Ladies (Alamo Square) & Fisherman's Wharf & Pier 39
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Painted Ladies (Alamo Square) — Row of Victorian houses with a skyline backdrop. In the afternoon, continue to Fisherman's Wharf & Pier 39 — Waterfront area famous for resident sea lions. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: San Francisco: Lombard Street & Twin Peaks viewpoint
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Lombard Street — "Crookedest street in the world" with eight hairpin turns. In the afternoon, continue to Twin Peaks viewpoint — Hilltop 360-degree panorama of the entire city. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Balmy Alley murals — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Mission District murals or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SFO for your departure flight.

travelTips: Pack warm layers regardless of season — the famous fog means summer days can be cooler than winter ones.
Visit Alcatraz early morning or evening for a less crowded ferry and cooler walking conditions on the island.
September-October, after fog season eases, is the best-kept-secret time to visit for clear, warm weather.
Ride the cable car on the Powell-Hyde line for the best hill views down to the bay.
Combine a day trip to Muir Woods with Sausalito for redwoods plus a scenic Bay ferry ride back.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Layers, including a warm jacket — "summer" can feel like winter due to fog
Comfortable, sturdy shoes for steep hills
A windbreaker for the often-breezy waterfront
Sunglasses for the clearer fall months
thingsToDo: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art (SFMOMA)
Cycling across the Golden Gate Bridge to Sausalito
Kayaking in the San Francisco Bay
Hiking the Land's End coastal trail
Golden Gate Park bike rentals and playgrounds
thingsToAvoid: The Tenderloin and parts of the Mid-Market area late at night — exercise normal urban caution.
Driving Lombard Street at peak midday — long queues form; walk it instead for a better experience.
Underdressing for "summer" — many visitors are caught off guard by the cold, foggy microclimate.
Leaving valuables visible in parked rental cars — break-ins at tourist parking areas (like near Golden Gate Bridge viewpoints) are common.
Skipping advance Alcatraz tickets — tours regularly sell out days or weeks ahead in peak season.
topRestaurants: Swan Oyster Depot — Seafood, counter-service institution ($$$)
House of Prime Rib — American Steakhouse ($$$$)
Boudin Bakery, Fisherman's Wharf — Sourdough Bread & Chowder ($)
State Bird Provisions — Modern American, dim-sum-style service ($$$$)
instagramSpots: Golden Gate Bridge from Battery Spencer
familyActivities: Golden Gate Park bike rentals and playgrounds
adventureActivities: Cycling across the Golden Gate Bridge to Sausalito
Kayaking in the San Francisco Bay
Hiking the Land's End coastal trail
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the Fairmont San Francisco on Nob Hill
Fine dining tasting menu at a Michelin-starred restaurant
Private wine-country day trip to Napa/Sonoma with a chauffeur
honeymoonExperiences: Romantic dinner in North Beach's Little Italy
Cable car ride together at dusk
Private sailing trip past the Golden Gate Bridge
budgetExperiences: Free Golden Gate Bridge vista points
Free walk through Golden Gate Park

festivalCalendar.name: Fleet Week
festivalCalendar.timing: October, air show over the Bay

localEtiquette: Be aware some downtown/Tenderloin blocks have visible homelessness — stay alert but respectful, and avoid judgmental behavior.
Cable cars and steep hills mean comfortable shoes are a practical necessity, not just a style choice.
San Francisco is generally casual — smart-casual dress is fine at most restaurants, even upscale ones.
localTransportGuide: San Francisco is compact and walkable but very hilly — the Muni system (buses, streetcars, cable cars) and BART regional rail cover most tourist areas well, and a Clipper card works across all of them. Rideshare apps (Uber/Lyft, both founded here) are widely used for hill-heavy or late-night trips.
bestTimeToVisit: September to November for the city's warmest, clearest weather (highs 20-23°C, after summer fog clears); summer (Jun-Aug) is notoriously cool and foggy despite being "peak season" elsewhere.
weather: Cool Mediterranean/maritime climate: mild, often foggy summers (highs just 18-19°C due to the marine layer, famously colder than spring/fall) and mild, wetter winters (highs 13-14°C, most of the year's rain falls Nov-Mar).
visaInformation: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview.
averageFlightTime: ~15h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit San Francisco?
faqs.answer: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit San Francisco?
faqs.answer: September to November for the city's warmest, clearest weather (highs 20-23°C, after summer fog clears); summer (Jun-Aug) is notoriously cool and foggy despite being "peak season" elsewhere. Cool Mediterranean/maritime climate: mild, often foggy summers (highs just 18-19°C due to the marine layer, famously colder than spring/fall) and mild, wetter winters (highs 13-14°C, most of the year's rain falls Nov-Mar).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is San Francisco safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: San Francisco is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to San Francisco?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Priya Garcia
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. de Young Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Dubois
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Exploratorium alone was worth the trip, but combined with de Young Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Lukas Smith
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: San Francisco is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Exploratorium. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
